article1.title: 10 Essential Tips for Driving in Morocco
article1.excerpt: Discover best practices and driving rules to drive safely on Moroccan roads.
content: Driving in Morocco is a unique experience that requires adaptation. Whether you're a tourist or resident, these tips will help you travel safely.
## Speed limits to respect
Maximum authorized speeds in Morocco are 120 km/h on highways, 100 km/h on national roads and 60 km/h in built-up areas (sometimes 40 km/h near schools).
Automatic radars are present on highways and some national roads. Fines are sent by mail to the vehicle owner.
## City driving
Urban traffic in Morocco has its particularities. Priorities are not always respected at intersections. Be particularly vigilant at roundabouts.
Two-wheelers often drive between lanes and can appear from anywhere. Always check your mirrors before changing lanes.
The horn is used frequently. A short beep to signal your presence is acceptable, but avoid aggressive honking.
## On rural roads
Country roads can present surprises: animals (sheep, goats, donkeys), carts, slow tractors, or pedestrians walking on the road.
Avoid driving at night on secondary roads. Street lighting is rare.
## Mandatory documents
Always keep with you: your driver's license (French or international accepted), vehicle registration, insurance certificate and rental contract.
## Parking
In cities, parking attendants are omnipresent. Give them 2 to 5 MAD for a few hours, or 5 to 10 MAD for a day. It's a common practice.
Never leave valuables visible in the car.
## Highways
The Moroccan highway network is modern and very well maintained. At the entrance, take a ticket at the toll. At the exit, you pay according to the distance traveled (about 0.80 MAD/km).
Service stations are present every 50-70 km with toilets, cafes and shops.
## In case of problem
If you have a breakdown or accident, immediately contact your rental agency. In case of accident with third parties, call the police (190).
Do not move vehicles before police arrive. Fill out an amicable report if possible and take photos.
## Fuel
Fill up at official stations (Total, Shell, Afriquia). Prices are fixed everywhere. An attendant will service you, a small tip of 2-5 MAD is appreciated.
On long trips to the desert, fill up as soon as possible because stations can be spaced 100 km or more apart.
## Last advice
Stay zen and patient. Moroccan driving style may seem chaotic at first, but by being attentive and careful, you will adapt quickly.

article2.title: The Most Beautiful Road Trips in Morocco by Car
article2.excerpt: From Marrakech to Merzouga, discover the must-see itineraries to explore Morocco freely.
content: Morocco is a country that is wonderfully discovered by car. Here are three tested and approved itineraries.
## Route 1: The Kasbah Route (Marrakech - Merzouga)
### Recommended duration: 4 to 5 days
### Total distance: about 850 km
This mythical itinerary takes you from Marrakech to the dunes of Merzouga through the Atlas and the southern valleys.
### Day 1: Marrakech - Ouarzazate (190 km)
Leave early from Marrakech to cross the Tizi n'Tichka pass (2,260m altitude). The road winds through the Atlas with spectacular panoramas.
Stop at the Aït Benhaddou kasbah, UNESCO listed, before arriving in Ouarzazate. Travel time: 4h with photo stops.
### Day 2: Ouarzazate - Dadès Gorges (110 km)
Cross the Rose Valley and the Dadès palm groves. The gorges offer breathtaking lunar landscapes.
### Day 3: Dadès - Merzouga (280 km)
Visit the Todra Gorges in the morning (300m high canyon), then head to Merzouga. Arrival in late afternoon for sunset.
### Recommended vehicle
An SUV or 4x4 is ideal for more comfort, but a sedan handles the main road very well.
## Route 2: The Atlantic Coast (Casablanca - Agadir)
### Recommended duration: 3 to 4 days
### Total distance: about 550 km
A relaxing route along the ocean, perfect for beach and seafood lovers.
### Day 1: Casablanca - El Jadida (100 km)
Visit Hassan II Mosque in the morning, then head to El Jadida and its fortified Portuguese city.
### Day 2: El Jadida - Essaouira (200 km)
Stop in Oualidia to taste fresh oysters. Arrival in Essaouira, coastal town with white and blue ramparts.
### Day 3: Essaouira - Agadir (175 km)
Take your time along the coast. Stop in small fishing villages.
### Recommended vehicle
A sedan or city car is more than enough. The roads are excellent.
## Route 3: The Mountainous North (Tangier - Fez)
### Recommended duration: 4 days
### Total distance: about 400 km
Discover the Rif, Chefchaouen the blue city, and the imperial medina of Fez.
### Day 1-2: Tangier - Chefchaouen (115 km)
Leave Tangier for the Rif mountains. Arrival in Chefchaouen, famous for its blue streets. Two nights recommended.
### Day 3-4: Chefchaouen - Fez (200 km)
Scenic route through the hills. Arrival in Fez in late afternoon to discover the world's largest medina.
### Recommended vehicle
Comfortable sedan. The roads are good but winding in the Rif.
## Practical tips
Spring and autumn are the best seasons. Book your accommodations in advance during high season. Bring water and snacks.

article3.title: Understanding Car Insurance in Morocco
article3.excerpt: Everything you need to know about insurance, deductibles and protection when renting.
content: Insurance is often a source of confusion when renting a car. Here's everything you need to know.
## Types of insurance available
### Third-party insurance (mandatory)
This is the minimum legal insurance in Morocco. It covers material damage caused to other vehicles and injuries caused to other people.
It does NOT cover damage to your rental vehicle. Price: generally included in the base rate.
### Comprehensive insurance (recommended)
This is the most complete option. It covers all damage to the rented vehicle (collision, crash), theft, fire, glass breakage and damage to third parties.
Price: supplement of 80 to 150 MAD per day depending on the vehicle. Our advice: always take comprehensive insurance.
## The deductible
### What is the deductible?
Even with comprehensive insurance, you remain responsible for part of the damage in case of loss.
### Typical amounts
Economy city car: 3,000 to 5,000 MAD. Sedan: 5,000 to 7,000 MAD. SUV: 7,000 to 10,000 MAD. Premium 4x4: 10,000 to 15,000 MAD.
### Deductible waiver option
Some agencies offer to reduce or eliminate the deductible for 50 to 100 MAD per day.
## What is covered
Traffic accidents, collision with another vehicle, crash with an obstacle, vehicle theft, fire, glass breakage.
Flat tire generally covered, spare wheel provided. Mechanical breakdown: breakdown assistance included.
## What is NOT covered
Gross negligence, drunk driving, driving by an undeclared person, undercarriage damage on unauthorized tracks.
Driving in wadis or flooded areas, use for racing, vehicle interior except fire.
## Necessary documents in case of accident
The amicable report provided by the agency in the glove box. To be filled out with the other driver if possible.
Take photos of the scene, damage, license plates. Note witnesses' contact information.
## Procedure in case of loss
1. Secure the area with hazard lights
2. Call the agency immediately
3. Call the police if necessary (190)
4. Fill out the report
5. Do not admit liability
## Pre-departure check
Walk around the vehicle completely with the agent. Note all small dents and scratches. Take photos of each side.
Check documents in the vehicle: registration card, insurance certificate, blank amicable report, warning triangle.
## Summary
Take comprehensive insurance (80-150 MAD/day). Check the vehicle carefully before leaving. Drive carefully. In case of problem, contact the agency.

article4.title: Car Rental Budget in Morocco: Complete Guide 2025
article4.excerpt: How much does a car rental cost in Morocco? Discover prices, tips and pitfalls to avoid.
content: Renting a car in Morocco is more affordable than in Europe. Here's a transparent and detailed guide.
## Rental prices by category
### Economy city car (Renault Clio, Dacia Sandero)
Low season: 200-280 MAD/day. Mid season: 280-350 MAD/day. High season: 350-450 MAD/day.
Ideal for city and short trips, 2 people with little luggage.
### Comfort sedan (Toyota Corolla, Renault Mégane)
Low season: 350-450 MAD/day. Mid season: 450-550 MAD/day. High season: 550-700 MAD/day.
Ideal for long trips, 4 people, superior comfort.
### Compact SUV (Dacia Duster, Peugeot 2008)
Low season: 450-600 MAD/day. Mid season: 600-750 MAD/day. High season: 750-1000 MAD/day.
Ideal for road trips, winding roads, 4-5 people with luggage.
### Premium 4x4 (Toyota Prado, Range Rover)
Low season: 900-1200 MAD/day. Mid season: 1200-1500 MAD/day. High season: 1500-2000 MAD/day.
Ideal for desert tracks, great comfort, groups of 5-6 people.
## Decreasing rates
Most agencies apply discounts for long rentals: 3-6 days -5 to -10%, 7-13 days -10 to -15%, 14-20 days -15 to -20%, 30 days and more -25 to -30%.
## Additional fees
### Comprehensive insurance
City car: +80-100 MAD/day. Sedan: +100-120 MAD/day. SUV/4x4: +120-150 MAD/day. Highly recommended.
### Additional driver
50-100 MAD/day depending on the agency. Sometimes free for spouse. The driver must have had their license for at least 1 year.
### Young driver (under 25)
Supplement of 50-100 MAD/day. License required for at least 2 years.
### Optional equipment
GPS: free or 30-50 MAD/day. Baby/child seat: 30-50 MAD/day.
### Delivery/pickup
Airport: often free. City hotel: 50-200 MAD. Between cities: 200-500 MAD. Return to another city: 500-1500 MAD.
## Fuel
### Current prices (January 2025)
Unleaded gasoline: about 13.50 MAD/liter. Diesel: about 11.50 MAD/liter. Fixed prices throughout Morocco.
### Average consumption
### Full to full rule
You pick up the vehicle with a full tank, you return it with a full tank. Fill up just before returning the vehicle.
## Highway tolls
Casablanca - Rabat: 20 MAD. Rabat - Tangier: 75 MAD. Casablanca - Marrakech: 65 MAD. About 0.30 to 0.35 MAD per kilometer.
## Parking
Informal attendants: 2-10 MAD/day. Official parking: 30-50 MAD/day. Blue zones: 2-5 MAD/hour. Average budget: 30-80 MAD/day.
## Complete budget examples
### Weekend in Marrakech (3 days)
City car: 900 MAD. Insurance: 270 MAD. Fuel: 150 MAD. Total: 1,350 MAD (about 125€).
### Merzouga road trip (7 days)
SUV: 5,355 MAD. Insurance: 910 MAD. Fuel: 2,100 MAD. Total: 8,515 MAD (about 800€).
### Full month digital nomad
Monthly sedan: 11,250 MAD. Insurance: 3,300 MAD. Fuel: 2,000 MAD. Total: 17,050 MAD (about 1,600€).
## Money-saving tips
1. Book 2-3 weeks in advance
2. Compare on Benatna to save 20-40%
3. Favor long rentals (decreasing rates)
4. Check what's included (unlimited mileage)
5. Fill up yourself before returning
6. Return the vehicle on time to avoid late fees

article5.title: Renting a Car in Morocco: The Complete Guide
article5.excerpt: Everything you need to know to rent a car in Morocco with peace of mind: documents, procedures, practical advice.
content: Parking in Morocco has its own codes. This guide explains how to park safely.
## Informal parking attendants
### Who are they?
Present in virtually all streets, parking attendants watch your vehicle in exchange for a tip. They usually wear a reflective vest.
### How does it work?
You park, an attendant approaches and helps you. He stays nearby and watches. Upon return, you give him a tip.
### How much to give?
A few hours: 2-5 MAD. Half a day: 5-10 MAD. All day: 10-20 MAD. Night: 20-30 MAD.
Tip: rather give 5 MAD than 2 MAD, it's minimal for you but significant for them.
### The right behavior
Be friendly. Accept their help to park if needed. Pay them when leaving, not when arriving. Have small change.
## Official monitored parking lots
### Where to find them?
Near shopping centers, near tourist sites, in business areas, around medinas.
### Average rates
First hour: 5-10 MAD. Additional hour: 3-5 MAD. Day rate: 30-60 MAD. Night: 20-40 MAD.
### Advantages
Maximum security with cameras and barriers. Ideal for long durations. Toilets often available.
## Blue zones (paid parking)
### What is it?
Paid parking zones delimited by blue markings, present in city centers.
### How to pay?
Either via parking meters, or via tickets sold by uniformed agents. Display the ticket behind the windshield.
### Rates
2-5 MAD per hour. Often limited to 2-4 hours maximum. Free on Sundays and after 7-8pm in some cities.
### If you don't pay?
Fine of 100-200 MAD. For a rental car, the agency will pay and charge you the fine plus management fees (100-200 MAD).
## Parking in medinas
### Golden rule: don't enter by car!
Medinas have narrow alleys, sometimes inaccessible to cars. You risk getting lost or stuck.
### Where to park?
Official parking lots are at the main entrance gates.
#### Marrakech
Jemaa el-Fna parking, Koutoubia parking. Rate: 30-50 MAD per day.
#### Fez
Bab Boujloud parking (blue gate), Bab Ftouh parking. Rate: 20-40 MAD per day.
#### Essaouira
Large parking at the medina entrance. Rate: 40-60 MAD/day.
You then explore the medina on foot.
## Rules to respect
### Where you MUST NOT park
In front of entrances, on pedestrian crossings, at bus stops, in front of fire hydrants, on curves, in front of mosques on Fridays.
### Parking on sidewalk
Tolerated in some narrow streets if a passage of at least 1 meter remains free for pedestrians. Observe how others are parked.
## Safety tips
### Before leaving your car
1. Don't leave anything visible
2. Close all doors and trunk
3. Check that windows are rolled up
4. Activate alarm if available
5. Keep important papers with you
### Car theft
Very rare in Morocco, especially for recent rental cars with alarm.
## Special cases
### Airport parking
Mohammed V Airport (Casablanca): 50-120 MAD/day depending on parking. Marrakech airport: 60-80 MAD/day.
### Hotel parking
3-4 star hotels: often free. Luxury hotels: 50-150 MAD/night.
With this guide, you'll know where and how to park everywhere in Morocco!

article6.title: Which Car to Choose for Your Stay in Morocco?
article6.excerpt: City car, SUV or 4x4: which vehicle best suits your travel program?
content: Understanding the fuel system in Morocco will help you better manage your budget.
## Types of fuel available
### Unleaded gasoline (Super)
Octane 95 or 98. Pump color: green. For most gasoline cars. Current price: about 13.50 MAD/liter.
Check on your contract or the vehicle's fuel flap.
### Diesel
Pump color: black or yellow. For diesel vehicles only. Current price: about 11.50 MAD/liter.
Warning: don't use the wrong fuel! Putting diesel in a gasoline car (or vice versa) is very expensive.
## Prices
### Fixed prices throughout Morocco
Prices are regulated by the state and identical in all stations. Whether in Casablanca or an isolated village, the price is the same.
No point looking for the cheapest station.
### Comparison with Europe
Fuel in Morocco is 20-30% cheaper than in France, 30-40% cheaper than in Belgium.
## The service station network
### Major brands
Total: densest network. Shell: many stations. Afriquia: Moroccan brand, very widespread. Winxo: recent stations. Petrom: present in the North.
All are reliable and offer the same fuel quality.
### Where to find them?
In city: very numerous, every 2-3 km. On highways: every 50-70 km. National roads: every 30-50 km. Rural areas: spaced 100 km or more.
## How does filling up work?
### Service by attendant
In Morocco, there is no self-service. An employee fills up for you.
You stop, the attendant approaches, you tell him what you want, he fills up, you pay and give a small tip (2-5 MAD).
### Useful phrases
Fill it up please = fill the tank. 100 dirhams of gasoline please = fixed amount. Specify unleaded gasoline or diesel if necessary.
### Payment methods
Cash: accepted everywhere. Bank card: accepted in most large stations. Always keep 200-300 MAD in cash.
## Planning your fill-ups
### In city
No problem, stations everywhere. Wait until the gauge drops to 1/4 before refilling.
### On highway
Service areas every 50-70 km. Very comfortable.
### National roads
Stations generally every 30-50 km. Don't let it drop below 1/4 tank.
### Towards desert and remote areas
Warning: distances between stations can reach 100-150 km.
Golden rule: Fill up as soon as you see a station if heading to the desert.
## The full to full rule
### How does it work?
The tank is full at pick-up. You must return it full as well. This is the standard rule.
### What to do on the last day?
30 minutes to 1h before returning the vehicle, find a nearby station and fill up completely. Keep the receipt.
### And if you forget?
The agency will charge you fuel at 15-18 MAD/L (instead of 13.50) plus management fees of 50-100 MAD. Total: 200-300 MAD more.
## Save fuel
### Adopt eco-driving
Accelerate gradually. Anticipate slowdowns. Maintain steady speed. Drive at 110 km/h instead of 120 km/h on highway.
### Air conditioning
AC consumes 0.5-1 L/100km extra. In city, open windows if bearable. On highway, use AC. Set to 22-24°C.
### Tire pressure
Under-inflated tires increase consumption by 5-10%. Check every 2 weeks. Inflation free or 2-5 MAD.
### Don't overload
Each extra 100 kg increases consumption by 5-7%. Only transport what's necessary.
## Summary

Check your car's fuel type. Fill up at official stations. Never let it drop below 1/4 tank. Fill up before desert areas. Fill up just before returning vehicle. Give 2-5 MAD tip to attendant. Always have 200-300 MAD in cash.

article7.title: Benatna: The Car Rental Revolution in Morocco
article7.excerpt: Discover how Benatna is transforming car rental in Morocco by connecting travelers with trusted local agencies.
content: Benatna is not just another car rental platform. It's a concrete answer to a problem that travelers and residents in Morocco face daily: how to rent a car with confidence, at a fair price, without unpleasant surprises?
## The observation: an opaque and fragmented market
For years, renting a car in Morocco was an obstacle course. Large international franchises charge high rates with hidden fees. Small local agencies, often excellent, remain invisible online.
Result: travelers pay too much at major brands, or take risks by booking through unsecured channels. Serious local agencies struggle to fill their fleet due to lack of visibility.
## Our mission: connect supply and demand
Benatna was born from a simple conviction: Morocco is full of professional, reliable and competitive rental agencies. We just need to make them visible and accessible.

Our platform brings together dozens of verified agencies in Morocco's main cities. Each partner is audited according to strict criteria: fleet condition, insurance coverage, rental process, customer reviews.
## Why rent on Benatna?
### 1. Fair and transparent prices

No hidden fees, no surprises when picking up your car. The displayed price is the final price, comprehensive insurance included. On average, our users save 20 to 40% compared to international franchises.
### 2. Verified local agencies
We only work with serious Moroccan agencies. Each partner signs a quality charter. In case of problem, our team intervenes to assist you. You benefit from local expertise without the risk.
### 3. 100% digital process
Book online in 3 minutes. Receive your confirmation by email. Pick up your vehicle without paperwork. Everything is already settled, you just take the keys and go.
No more spending 30 minutes at the counter filling out forms, negotiating additional insurance, or deciphering contracts in small print.
### 4. Wide choice of vehicles
Over 300 cars available in 6 cities. Economy city cars to explore the city, comfortable SUVs for road trips, 4x4s for the desert. All recent, well maintained, equipped with AC and GPS.
### 5. Responsive customer support
A question before booking? A problem during your rental? Our team based in Morocco responds in French, English and Arabic. WhatsApp, email, phone: we're here for you.
No outsourced call center that doesn't know the field. Our team lives in Morocco, knows the cities, roads, agencies. We respond quickly with concrete solutions.
### 6. Unlimited mileage
Explore Morocco without counting kilometers. All our rentals include unlimited mileage. Marrakech, Merzouga desert, Essaouira beaches, Chefchaouen blue city: travel the country freely.
## Who are our partners?
### Family agencies for 20 years
Most of our partners are family businesses established for decades. They know the business, maintain their fleet carefully, and consider their reputation their greatest capital.
### Certified professionals
All our partners have official licenses issued by Moroccan authorities. Their vehicles are regularly inspected. Their insurance is up to date and compliant.
### Present in main cities
Our agencies cover Casablanca, Marrakech, Rabat, Tangier, Agadir, Fez and their airports. Wherever you land or stay, a Benatna partner is nearby.
## Benatna in numbers

Since our launch in early 2024: Over 1,200 satisfied customers. 24 verified partner agencies. 302 vehicles available. 6 cities covered. 98% customer satisfaction. Average savings of 30% vs international franchises.
## In conclusion
Benatna is not perfect. We're a young startup, we learn every day, we make mistakes. But we have an obsession: make car rental in Morocco simple, transparent and accessible to all.
We believe in the potential of local agencies. We believe Morocco deserves a modern and reliable platform. We believe travelers and agencies can win together.
If you share this vision, welcome to Benatna. Mobility, between us.
